etymology.description: 繁(繁) (fán, "complex; numerous") + 體(體) (tǐ, "body; style") form the compound word 繁體 (fántǐ), referring to the traditional and complex style of Chinese characters, as opposed to simplified characters.
etymology.interpretation: 繁(繁) (fán, "complex; numerous") indicates the intricate nature of traditional characters, which have numerous strokes. 體(體) (tǐ, "body; style") refers to the form or style of these characters. The combination conveys the concept of the traditional orthography, emphasizing its complexity and detailed form compared to the streamlined simplified characters.

examples.chinese: 很多书籍使用繁体字(很多書籍使用繁體字)。
examples.english: Many books use traditional Chinese characters.

examples.chinese: 繁体字在香港和台湾广泛使用(繁體字在香港和臺灣廣泛使用)。
examples.pinyin: Fántǐzì zài Xiānggǎng hé Táiwān guǎngfàn shǐyòng.
examples.english: Traditional Chinese characters are widely used in Hong Kong and Taiwan.

examples.chinese: 有些人觉得书写繁体字很难(有些人覺得書寫繁體字很難)。
examples.pinyin: Yǒuxiē rén juéde shūxiě fántǐzì hěn nán.
examples.english: Some people find writing traditional Chinese characters very difficult.
